trajectory.description: Observation: Empty workspace, 10h budget, 30 iterations. Need to quickly validate pipeline for Minigrid RL. Analysis: Using SB3 PPO with a simple flat observation wrapper (image + direction) is the fastest way to get a working baseline. It avoids dict/CNN complexities and trains a small MLP. Decision: Implement wrappers.py, train.py, predict.py. Run a short 200k-step training to measure iteration time and validate predict.py, then submit eval immediately.

Observation: The previous training failed because JsonlMetricsCallback inherits from BaseCallback but does not implement the abstract _on_step method, causing a TypeError on instantiation. Analysis: This is a simple code bug; adding a _on_step method that returns True will fix it. The wrappers and predict.py look correct for a flat-observation MLP PPO baseline. Decision: Fix train.py by implementing _on_step, then rerun a short 200k-step training and immediately submit eval to validate the full pipeline and measure iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completed successfully in ~124s for 200k timesteps. Model saved to checkpoints/iter_1/ppo_minigrid.zip. Training curves are empty because SB3 logger keys don't match 'loss'/'accuracy', but this is cosmetic for now. Analysis: The pipeline is functional. I need to validate predict.py and measure evaluation time by submitting an eval run. This will establish a baseline success_rate for the flat-observation MLP PPO model. Decision: Submit evaluation immediately using the iter_1 checkpoint. No code changes needed.

Observation: The baseline MLP PPO with flat observations scored 0% success on all 500 eval episodes. Analysis: Flattening the 7x7x3 image into a 151-dim vector and training for only 200k timesteps is highly suboptimal for Minigrid — spatial structure is destroyed and each env received just ~20k steps. Additionally, dividing raw categorical indices by 255 squashes informative signals. Decision: Switch to an embedding-based feature extractor that treats each image channel (object, color, state) as categorical and maps them through nn.Embedding before an MLP. Return observations as (3,7,7) float32 to avoid SB3's automatic /255 normalization. Place the custom extractor in a separate importable module (model.py) so SB3 can reload it in predict.py. Train for 1M timesteps and evaluate.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completed in ~457s (7.6 min) for 1M timesteps with the custom MinigridExtractor. No eval has been run yet for this checkpoint. Analysis: I need to measure whether the embedding-based CNN/MLP extractor improved the 0% success rate from the flat-observation baseline. The training time is manageable (~8 min per 1M steps), so depending on the result I can scale up timesteps, adjust architecture, or add curriculum learning. Decision: Submit evaluation for the iter_4 checkpoint immediately to get a baseline score.

trajectory.command:
python train.py && python -c "
import gymnasium as gym, minigrid
from stable_baselines3 import PPO
from wrappers import ImgObsWrapper
from model import MinigridExtractor
import os
iter_num = os.environ.get('RAB_ITERATION', '0')
env = gym.make('MiniGrid-LavaGapS7-v0')
env = ImgObsWrapper(env)
obs, _ = env.reset(seed=0)
model = PPO.load(f'checkpoints/iter_{iter_num}/ppo_minigrid.zip')
a, _ = model.predict(obs, deterministic=True)
print('Local predict test passed, action:', a)
"
trajectory.command_output_summary:
Model saved to checkpoints/iter_6
Local predict test passed, action: 2
trajectory.description: Observation: Iter_4 checkpoint achieved 13% success rate (up from 0%). Only LavaGapS7 (100%) and LavaCrossingS9N2 (28%) saw any success; all other environments failed completely. Analysis: The previous architecture flattened the 7x7 spatial grid into an MLP, destroying spatial structure. It also discarded the 'direction' signal, which is essential for navigation in an egocentric partial-view grid. Decision: 1) Return observations as a Dict with 'image' (channel-first) and 'direction' (one-hot). 2) Switch to MultiInputPolicy with a custom CNN feature extractor that preserves spatial structure after embedding object/color/state indices. 3) Increase capacity (features_dim=256, net_arch=[256,128]). 4) Add entropy coefficient 0.01 to encourage exploration. 5) Train for 5M timesteps (~38 min based on 1M ≈ 7.6 min). This should significantly improve harder environments like DoorKey, FourRooms, and MultiRoom.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 6 completed training in ~42 minutes (5M timesteps) with the new Dict-observation CNN extractor. No evaluation score is available yet for this checkpoint. The training curves are empty because SB3 logs don't contain 'loss' or 'accuracy' keys, but this doesn't affect model performance. Analysis: I need the evaluation score for iter_6 before deciding on further architectural or hyperparameter changes. The training time was significant (~42 min), so I must know whether the CNN + spatial preservation improved upon the 13% success rate from iter_4. Decision: Submit evaluation immediately for the iter_6 checkpoint without any code changes.

Observation: Iter_6 achieved 44.2% success rate with the Dict-observation CNN extractor (5M steps). Several environments remain at 0% (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom) and FourRooms is only 12%. These are larger, harder exploration tasks. Analysis: The policy likely lacks temporal memory and sufficient capacity/model size to solve multi-room/key tasks. Increasing network size (features_dim 256→512, net_arch [512,256]) and adding a 4-frame observation stack will provide short-term memory (motion, door toggling) while keeping total parameters well under 10M. I will also increase n_steps to 4096 for lower-variance updates on longer episodes, and train for 15M total timesteps (~2.5h based on 5M≈42min). This should push success rate significantly higher. Decision: Update wrappers.py to add FrameStackWrapper, update model.py to handle stacked channels, update train.py and predict.py to use the new wrapper, then launch a 15M-step training run.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter_8 achieved 49.6% success rate (new best). Four environments remain at 0% (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom) and FourRooms is only 24%. These are large, sparse-reward environments requiring long-horizon memory and exploration. Analysis: The current frame stack of 4 provides only ~4 steps of memory, insufficient for multi-room navigation. Additionally, I need to ensure SB3 does not normalize image observations before they reach my embedding-based extractor (normalize_images=False). To improve exploration in hard tasks, I will: 1) increase frame_stack to 8 for more temporal context, 2) raise entropy coefficient to 0.03, 3) double n_steps to 8192 for lower-variance updates on longer episodes, 4) increase batch_size to 1024, 5) set gamma=0.995 for longer credit assignment, 6) train for 30M steps (~4h). Decision: Update train.py, predict.py, and wrappers.py with these hyperparameters and submit a long training run.

Observation: Iteration 10 crashed immediately with TypeError because normalize_images=False was passed to PPO instead of inside policy_kwargs. All other changes (8-frame stack, ent_coef=0.03, gamma=0.995, n_steps=8192, batch_size=1024, 30M steps) are sound. Analysis: Moving normalize_images into policy_kwargs fixes the crash. Additionally, replacing DummyVecEnv with SubprocVecEnv will parallelize the 10 training environments across the 24 CPU cores, cutting wall-clock time by several fold and allowing the 30M-step run to finish safely within the remaining ~7 hours. Decision: Update train.py only—fix the argument placement and switch to SubprocVecEnv—then launch the 30M-step training run.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 11 completed successfully after ~4.4 hours of training (30M timesteps) with the enhanced architecture (8-frame stack, ent_coef=0.03, gamma=0.995, n_steps=8192, batch_size=1024, SubprocVecEnv). The model was saved to checkpoints/iter_11/ppo_minigrid.zip. The previous best success rate was 49.6% from iter_8 (15M steps). Analysis: Doubling training steps and increasing exploration entropy may have improved policy performance on the hard zero-success environments (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom, FourRooms), or it may have caused instability. With only ~2.6 hours remaining, I cannot afford another long training run without first measuring iter_11's performance. Decision: Submit evaluation immediately for the iter_11 checkpoint to see if the 30M-step run improved upon the 49.6% baseline.

Observation: Iter_11 (30M steps, ent_coef=0.03) scored 49.4% success, slightly below the best 49.6% from iter_8 (15M steps, ent_coef=0.01). Four environments remain at 0% (KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom) and LavaCrossing regressed from 90% to 86%, indicating excessive exploration entropy. Analysis: The policy has plateaued because hard tasks get too few samples and too little gradient signal in a uniform 10-env mix, while high entropy hurts already-solved tasks. Decision: 1) Oversample hard envs (3x copies of KeyCorridor, LockedRoom, UnlockPickup, MultiRoom; 2x FourRooms) to increase their training signal. 2) Revert entropy to 0.01 for better exploitation. 3) Increase capacity to features_dim=1024 and net_arch=[1024,512] (still well under 10M params). 4) Raise n_epochs to 10 for better sample efficiency and lower clip_range to 0.1 for stability. 5) Keep n_steps=4096 for fast updates. 6) Train for 10M timesteps (~1.5h expected), fitting safely within the 2.6h budget and leaving time for eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter_13 severely regressed to 12.4% success (down from best 49.6% in iter_8). Analysis: The oversampled large-network setup diverged, destroying performance on previously solved tasks. With only ~1 hour remaining, the safest way to improve the 49.6% best score is to continue training from the stable iter_8 checkpoint (4-frame stack, 512-dim features, [512,256] MLP, 15M steps) rather than risk another unstable config. Adding ~5M more steps should take ~40 minutes, fitting in the remaining budget. Decision: Update train.py to support loading iter_8 via --load_path and continuing PPO training with identical 4-frame stack wrappers. Update predict.py to read a saved config.json so it uses the correct frame_stack for the new checkpoint. Launch 5M-step continuation and then evaluate.
